name: Abdomen & Pelvis Ultrasound (KUB)
description: Comprehensive ultrasound examination of the abdomen and pelvis including kidneys, ureters, and bladder. Detects stones, cysts, tumors, and organ abnormalities.
prepInstructions: Fast for 6 hours before the test. Drink 4–6 glasses of water 1 hour before and do not urinate until after the scan.

name: Obstetric Anatomy Scan (2D/3D)
description: Detailed fetal anatomy scan performed between 18–22 weeks of pregnancy. Evaluates fetal growth, organ development, amniotic fluid, and placental position.
prepInstructions: Drink 2–3 glasses of water 30 minutes before the scan. Do not empty your bladder before the appointment.

name: Carotid Doppler Imaging
description: Doppler ultrasound of the carotid arteries to assess blood flow and detect plaque buildup, stenosis, or risk of stroke.
prepInstructions: No special preparation required. Wear a loose-fitting shirt or top with a low neckline for easy access to the neck area.

name: Thyroid & Neck Ultrasound
description: High-resolution ultrasound of the thyroid gland and surrounding neck structures. Identifies nodules, goiter, cysts, and lymph node enlargement.
prepInstructions: No preparation needed. Wear a shirt with a low or open neckline. Remove any necklaces before the appointment.

name: Echocardiogram (Heart USG)
description: Ultrasound imaging of the heart to evaluate cardiac structure and function, including valves, chambers, and ejection fraction.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. Avoid heavy exercise 2 hours before the test. Wear a two-piece outfit for easy chest access.

name: Liver & Gallbladder Ultrasound
description: Focused ultrasound of the liver and gallbladder to detect fatty liver, cirrhosis, gallstones, polyps, and bile duct dilation.
prepInstructions: Fast for at least 6 hours before the scan. You may drink plain water. Avoid fatty foods the evening before.

name: Pelvic Ultrasound (Female)
description: Transabdominal and transvaginal ultrasound to evaluate the uterus, ovaries, and fallopian tubes. Detects fibroids, cysts, PCOS, and endometriosis.
prepInstructions: Drink 4 glasses of water 1 hour before the scan and do not urinate. For transvaginal scan, an empty bladder is preferred.

name: Scrotal Ultrasound
description: Ultrasound examination of the testes and epididymis to evaluate pain, swelling, varicocele, hydrocele, or suspected torsion.
prepInstructions: No special preparation required. Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing.

name: Musculoskeletal (MSK) Ultrasound
description: Dynamic ultrasound of joints, tendons, ligaments, and muscles. Useful for diagnosing rotator cuff tears, tendinitis, bursitis, and joint effusions.
prepInstructions: No preparation required. Wear loose clothing that allows easy access to the area being examined.

name: Renal Doppler Ultrasound
description: Doppler assessment of blood flow in the renal arteries and veins. Evaluates hypertension, renal artery stenosis, and transplant kidney function.
prepInstructions: Fast for 4–6 hours before the scan. Drink water as needed to stay hydrated.

name: Breast Ultrasound
description: High-frequency ultrasound of breast tissue to characterize lumps, cysts, and masses. Often used alongside mammography for comprehensive evaluation.
prepInstructions: No preparation required. Avoid applying deodorant, lotion, or powder to the breast area on the day of the scan.

name: Prostate Ultrasound (TRUS)
description: Transrectal ultrasound of the prostate gland to measure size, detect nodules, and guide biopsy procedures. Evaluates BPH and prostate cancer risk.
prepInstructions: An enema may be required 2 hours before the procedure. Follow the specific instructions provided at the time of booking.

name: Complete Blood Count (CBC)
description: Measures red blood cells, white blood cells, hemoglobin, hematocrit, and platelets. Essential for diagnosing anemia, infections, and blood disorders.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. Inform the technician of any medications you are currently taking.

name: Lipid Profile (Cholesterol Panel)
description: Measures total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, and triglycerides. Used to assess cardiovascular risk and monitor lipid-lowering therapy.
prepInstructions: Fast for 9–12 hours before the test. You may drink plain water. Avoid alcohol for 24 hours prior.

name: Fasting Blood Sugar (FBS)
description: Measures blood glucose after an overnight fast. Primary screening test for diabetes mellitus and prediabetes.
prepInstructions: Fast for 8–10 hours before the test. Drink only plain water. Do not take diabetes medications until after the blood draw.

name: HbA1c (Glycated Hemoglobin)
description: Reflects average blood glucose levels over the past 2–3 months. Used to diagnose and monitor long-term diabetes management.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. This test can be done at any time of day.

name: Liver Function Tests (LFTs)
description: Panel of tests measuring liver enzymes (ALT, AST, ALP), bilirubin, and albumin to assess liver health and detect hepatitis, cirrhosis, or drug toxicity.
prepInstructions: Fast for 8 hours before the test. Avoid alcohol for at least 24 hours prior to the blood draw.

name: Kidney Function Tests (KFTs)
description: Measures creatinine, urea, uric acid, and electrolytes to evaluate kidney function and detect chronic kidney disease or acute kidney injury.
prepInstructions: Stay well hydrated. Fast for 8 hours if combined with other tests. Avoid strenuous exercise the day before.

name: Thyroid Function Tests (TFTs)
description: Measures TSH, T3, and T4 levels to diagnose hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and monitor thyroid replacement therapy.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. If on thyroid medication, take it after the blood draw unless instructed otherwise by your doctor.

name: Urine Complete Examination (UCE)
description: Microscopic and chemical analysis of urine to detect infections, kidney disease, diabetes, and other metabolic conditions.
prepInstructions: Collect a midstream urine sample in the provided sterile container. Avoid urinating for at least 2 hours before collection.

name: Hepatitis B Surface Antigen (HBsAg)
description: Detects the presence of Hepatitis B virus surface antigen in blood. Used for screening, diagnosis, and monitoring of Hepatitis B infection.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. Inform the technician of any recent vaccinations.

name: Vitamin D (25-OH) Test
description: Measures 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels in blood to diagnose deficiency or toxicity. Important for bone health, immunity, and metabolic function.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. This test can be done at any time of day.

name: Iron Studies Panel
description: Measures serum iron, ferritin, TIBC, and transferrin saturation to diagnose iron deficiency anemia, iron overload, and anemia of chronic disease.
prepInstructions: Fast for 8–12 hours before the test. Do not take iron supplements for 24 hours prior to the blood draw.

name: C-Reactive Protein (CRP)
description: Measures CRP levels as a marker of systemic inflammation. Used to detect infections, autoimmune conditions, and cardiovascular risk.
prepInstructions: No fasting required. Inform the technician if you have had any recent infections or are taking anti-inflammatory medications.

name: Premium Executive Health Package
description: Comprehensive executive health screening including full blood panel, cardiac evaluation, abdominal ultrasound, and thyroid assessment. Ideal for annual corporate health checks.
prepInstructions: Fast for 10–12 hours before your appointment. Drink only plain water. Bring any previous medical reports. Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing.

name: Women's Wellness Package
description: Tailored health screening for women including pelvic ultrasound, breast ultrasound, CBC, thyroid panel, vitamin D, and iron studies.
prepInstructions: Fast for 8 hours before the appointment. Drink water to keep your bladder full for the pelvic scan. Wear a two-piece outfit.

name: Cardiac Risk Assessment Package
description: Comprehensive cardiac screening including echocardiogram, carotid doppler, lipid profile, CRP, blood sugar, and ECG. Recommended for patients with family history of heart disease.
prepInstructions: Fast for 10 hours before the appointment. Avoid caffeine and strenuous exercise 24 hours prior. Bring a list of current medications.

name: Diabetes Management Package
description: Complete diabetes monitoring panel including FBS, HbA1c, kidney function tests, lipid profile, urine examination, and abdominal ultrasound.
prepInstructions: Fast for 10–12 hours before the appointment. Do not take diabetes medications until after the blood draw. Drink water to stay hydrated.

name: Antenatal Care Package
description: Complete antenatal screening including obstetric anatomy scan, CBC, blood group & Rh typing, urine examination, hepatitis B, and thyroid function tests.
prepInstructions: Drink 3–4 glasses of water 1 hour before the scan. Do not empty your bladder. Bring your previous antenatal records.

name: Basic Health Screening Package
description: Affordable entry-level health check including CBC, blood sugar, urine examination, and abdominal ultrasound. Suitable for routine annual screening.
prepInstructions: Fast for 8 hours before the appointment. Drink water to keep your bladder full for the ultrasound. Wear comfortable clothing.
